---
title: "03-Results"
Author: "Mikkel E. Steentoft"
format: html
editor: visual
theme: cosmo
bibliography: references.bib
csl: acta-diabetologica.csl
---

## Study population

In total, 1,385 participants were included in the clustering analysis. The included study population has a mean age of 66 years (SD = 7.1) and 53% were male. The mean BMI was 27.1 kg/m² (SD = 4.5) and the majority were current smokers (63%). Glycemic categories were distributed as follows: 735 (53%) NGT, 249 (18%) iIFG, 122 (8.8%) iIGT, 117 (8.4%) IFG+IGT and 162 (12%) type 2 diabetes. Of the 1,385 included in the clustering analysis, 162 were excluded due to missing data on VAT, SAT, smoking status or physical activity, leaving n = 1,223 participants for the regression analysis. Participants excluded from the regression analysis were more likely to have type 2 diabetes and impaired glucose tolerance and has higher BMI compared to the included participants. Details of the exclusion process are shown in figure 1.

\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_

#### Flow-chart figure 1 \_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_

## K-means clustering

### Clustering solution and characteristics

A three-cluster solution was identified as the best clustering solution based on a combination of statistical and biological criteria. While the NbClust package indicated highest support for two and nine clusters, a two-cluster solution collapsed the incretin and glucagon heterogeneity into a single group dominated by glucose and insulin differences, and solutions with more than three clusters produced very small clusters (n=2) driven by extreme values on glucagon suppression. The three-cluster solution represented the best combination of statistical quality, with a silhouette width of 0.21, and biological interpretability. Cluster characteristics are presented in table 1. Cluster 1 (n = 461) has the youngest mean age (64.8 years), highest proportion of males (62%) and highest physical activity level (53.5 kj/kg/day), while cluster 2 (n = 491) has the highest proportion of females (59%) and cluster 3 (n = 433) has the oldest mean age (66.9 years) and highest proportion of current smokers (68%). Regarding adiposity, cluster 3 has the highest BMI (29.1 kg/m²), waist circumference (101.4 cm), body fat percentage (34.7%) and VAT (9.4 cm), while cluster 2 has the lowest BMI (25.8 kg/m²) and waist circumference (90.8 cm). The glycemic status distribution is markedly different across clusters, with cluster 3 having the highest proportion of type 2 diabetes (33%), IFG+IGT (25%) and iIGT (21%), compared to clusters 1 and 2 where the majority have normoglycemia (70% and 78%, respectively).

\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_

#### Cluster characteristics table 1 \_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_

OGTT response patterns across clusters are plotted in figure 2, showing mean response curves for glucose, insulin, GLP-1, GIP and glucagon (*Fig. 2A, B, C, D* and *E,* respectively) at all three time points (0, 30 and 120 minutes).

\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_

#### Response pattern figure 2 \_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_

Cluster 3 is characterized by elevated glucose levels throughout the OGTT, with the highest fasting glucose (6.5 mmol/L), and 2hPG (9.1 mmol/L) alongside the highest insulin concentrations, with a fasting insulin of 53.6 pmol/L and 30 minute and 2-hour insulin levels of 249 pmol/L and 387.2 pmol/L, respectively. This corresponds to the lowest value on the insulin sensitivity index across clusters (ISI = 21). In contrast, cluster 2 has the lowest 2hPG (5.4 mmol/L) and a higher peak in insulin at 30 minutes (214 pmol/L) compared to cluster 1 (180 pmol/L), with a corresponding highest value on the insulin sensitivity index of 48 compared to 42 for cluster 1. Cluster 1 has the lowest incretin secretion (AUC<sub>0-120</sub> = 1,873 and 3,865 pmol/L × min for GLP-1 and GIP, respectively), almost half of the incretin secretion of cluster 2 (AUC<sub>0-120</sub> = 3,938 and 6,265 pmol/L × min for GLP-1 and GIP, respectively). Furthermore, cluster 3 showed an early increase in glucagon (0-30 min suppression = -10.3%), but the highest late glucagon suppression (30-120 min, 44.6%), and cluster 2 has lower glucagon suppression than cluster 1 across all intervals. Despite the differences in early and late response patterns, cluster 1 and 3 have almost similar overall glucagon suppression (47% and 45.4%, respectively), with cluster 3 having the highest glucagon secretion (AUC <sub>0-120 min</sub> = 1,085 pmol/L × min). Detailed OGTT characteristics are shown in table 2.

\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_

#### OGTT characteristics table 2 \_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_

### Associations to measures of fat distribution

Estimates from all four outcome models and step-wise adjustments are shown in table 3. In the fully adjusted model, both clusters 1 and 2 has lower VAT relative to cluster 3 (cluster 1: -21%, 95%CI: -24.2%, -17.7%; cluster 2: -20.7%, 95%CI: -23.8%, -17.4%). In contrast, clusters 1 and 2 are markedly different on SAT, where cluster 2 has 12.3% lower SAT (95%CI: -17.6, -6.7) and cluster 1 showed no significant difference (-1.4%, 95%CI: -7.5, 5.0). This difference is reflected in the VAT/SAT ratio, with cluster 1 showing a larger reduction (-19.9%) than cluster 2 (-9.5%). Both clusters has lower body fat percentage relative to cluster 3, with cluster 2 showing a slightly larger reduction (-5.12 percentage points) than cluster 1 (-3.94 percentage points). Sex-specific associations are illustrated in figure 3. Interaction-terms were non-significant in all models, with the most pronounced sex differences observed in the VAT/SAT ratio and SAT for cluster 1. Estimates were largely unchanged after further adjustment for smoking and physical activity.

\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_

#### Forest plot of sex-differences in associations figure 3 \_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_
